understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to get a pushable object (block) onto a goal tile marked with '+' on the semantic grid. Each level required navigating the puzzle by moving selectable pieces and potentially pushing blocks to their target positions.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (one grid cell). CLICK with target_position allowed switching between multiple selectable pieces on the board. Pushable blocks could be moved by the selected piece if adjacent. Walls blocked movement and had to be navigated around. The semantic_grid showed P (selected piece), S (other selectables), B (blocks), # (walls), + (goals), and . (empty cells). The level had a strict step budget that counted down with each action. Despite many attempts in the final level, we ran out of steps before achieving the WIN condition, suggesting the puzzle required a more efficient solution path or different strategy.

understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement used MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (one grid cell). CLICK with target_position allowed switching between multiple selectable pieces on the board. Pushable blocks could be moved by the selected piece if adjacent. Walls blocked movement and had to be navigated around. The semantic_grid showed P (selected piece), S (other selectables), B (blocks), # (walls), + (goals), and . (empty cells). The level had a strict step budget that counted down with each action. Despite many attempts in the final level, we ran out of steps before achieving the WIN condition, suggesting the puzzle required a more efficient solution path or different strategy.
